children.text: The term “best value” means, with respect to an acquisition, the optimal combination of cost, quality, technical capability or solution quality, and delivery schedule.
The term “capability requirement” means a capability that is critical or essential to address an operational problem.
The term “cost-effective” means, with respect to an acquisition, delivering superior end-user results at equal or lower cost compared to alternatives.

children.label: A
children.text: a challenge of a military department in achieving an assigned military objective based on current doctrine, emerging threats, or future concepts; and

children.label: B
children.text: may include limitations in capabilities, capacity, resources, or the ability to effectively and efficiently coordinate across the joint force, with another combatant command, or among military capabilities.

children.label: A
children.text: the Chief of Staff of the Army, with respect to matters concerning the Army;

children.label: B
children.text: the Chief of Naval Operations, with respect to matters concerning the Navy;

children.label: C
children.text: the Commandant of the Marine Corps, with respect to matters concerning the Marine Corps;

children.label: D
children.text: the Chief of Staff of the Air Force, with respect to matters concerning the Air Force; and

children.label: E
children.text: the Chief of Space Operations, with respect to matters concerning the Space Force.
